NOTICE OF MOTION AND OITON TO APPROVE PROP 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO ANTHROPOLOGIE, INC. FILED BY PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION HEARING SET FOR JUL-31-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO ANTHROPOLOGIE, INCORPORATED IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. (1) THE MATERIALS SUPPLIED ARE INSUFFICIENT TO APPROVE THE REASONABLENESS OF THE ATTORNEYS FEES. SPECIFICALLY, WHILE THERE IS A BASIS FOR THE HOURLY RATE, THERE IS NOTHING TO SUGGEST THAT THE HOURS SPENT ON E.G. 'LITIGATION' (OR ANY OTHER CATEGORY) WERE REASONABLE. SIMPLY BREAKING DOWN HOURS INTO LARGE CATEGORIES SUCH AS 'LITIGATION,' 'INVESTIGATION' AND 'POST SETTLEMENT' DOES NOT PERMIT ANY JUDGMENT OF REASONABLENESS. (2) IT APPEARS THAT THE TOTAL FINES ARE $18,000. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES THE CORRECT PERCENTAGE OF THAT SUM (75%) TO THE STATE. (3) IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT RELEASING LANGUAGE IN THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT STATES THAT NO CLAIMS ARISING AFTER THE DATE OF THE JUDGMENT ARE RELEASED. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: FREDRIC EVENSON, ESQ., LAW OFFICES OF FREDRIC EVENSON, 831.454.8216, FOR PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION.
ANNE KEARNS, ESQ., KRIEG KELLER SLOAN REILLEY & ROMAN LLP, 415.249.8330, FOR DEFENDANT ANTHROPOLOGIE, INC.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement and for entry of settlement agreement as to Anthropologie, Inc. IS GRANTED. The settlement is approved with revised language providing no admission of liability by Defendants. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-18-2012 Continued To Dec-10-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Oiton To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Anthropologie, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION'S MOTION To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Anthropologie, Incorporated is Denied without prejudice. (1) The materials supplied are insufficient to approve the reasonableness of the attorneys fees. Specifically, while there is a basis for the hourly rate, there is nothing to suggest that the hours spent on e.g. 'litigation' (or any other category) were reasonable. Simply breaking down hours into large categories such as 'litigation,' 'investigation' and 'post settlement' does not permit any judgment of reasonableness. (2) It appears that the total fines are $18,000. It does not appear that the agreement provides the correct percentage of that sum (75%) to the state. (3) It does not appear that releasing language in the settlement agreement states that no claims arising after the date of the judgment are released. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Set For Trial On Aug-06-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
